entry.title: Azure Archive Storage
entry.description: Azure Archive Storage provides low-cost, long-term cloud storage for rarely accessed data with high durability and security.

freeTierDetails.summary: 5 GB of Archive Storage free per month for 12 months.
caveats: Free tier valid for 12 months
Retrieval and early deletion fees apply
billingRiskNotes: Retrieval and early deletion can incur unexpected charges

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure account and activate the free tier.
Create a Storage Account in the Azure Portal.
Add a new Blob container and set its access tier to Archive.
Upload files to the container using Azure Portal, CLI, or SDK.
Monitor usage and manage retrievals to avoid unexpected costs.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need to store large amounts of infrequently accessed data at low cost for long-term retention.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require frequent or low-latency access, or need instant data retrieval.
entry.body: Azure Archive Storage is designed for organizations needing secure, durable, and cost-effective storage for data that is rarely accessed but must be retained for long periods. The free tier allows limited archival storage for up to 12 months, making it suitable for testing or small-scale archival needs. Retrieval times are slow and may incur additional costs, so it's best for compliance, backup, or regulatory scenarios where immediate access is not required.

entry.title: Azure Blob Storage
entry.description: Scalable object storage for unstructured data such as images, videos, backups, and logs on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: 5 GB LRS hot block blob storage and 20,000 read/write operations per month free.
caveats: Only applies to locally redundant storage (LRS) in hot tier.
Additional usage incurs charges.
billingRiskNotes: Charges accrue if you exceed free tier limits.
No hard cap—monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal and create a new Storage Account.
Select 'BlobStorage' as the account kind and choose the 'Hot' access tier.
Create a new container within your storage account.
Upload files to the container using the portal or Azure CLI.
Configure access policies or generate SAS tokens as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need scalable, reliable object storage for unstructured data with global access and integration with Azure services.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need a hard usage cap, require on-premises storage, or want a free tier without a credit card.
entry.body: Azure Blob Storage provides scalable, secure object storage for a wide range of unstructured data, with a free tier suitable for small projects and testing.

entry.title: Azure Disk Storage
entry.description: Azure Disk Storage provides scalable, high-performance block storage for Azure virtual machines and workloads.

freeTierDetails.summary: Azure offers 32 GB of Standard SSD Managed Disk storage free per month for 12 months.
limits: 32 GB Standard SSD Managed Disk per month
caveats: Only available to new Azure accounts
Charges apply after free limit or 12 months
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding the free limit or continuing after 12 months incurs charges

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Azure account and activate the free tier.
Navigate to the Azure Portal and select 'Create a resource'.
Search for and select 'Managed Disks'.
Configure disk size (up to 32 GB Standard SSD for free tier) and settings.
Attach the disk to a virtual machine or use as standalone storage.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need reliable, persistent block storage for Azure virtual machines or workloads with moderate performance needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need object storage, require more than 32 GB free, or plan to run outside Azure's ecosystem.
entry.body: Azure Disk Storage offers managed, persistent block storage for Azure VMs, with a 32 GB Standard SSD free tier for 12 months for new users.

entry.title: Azure File Storage
entry.description: Azure File Storage provides fully managed file shares accessible via SMB and NFS protocols in the cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: 5 GB standard performance file shares per month free in general-purpose v2 storage accounts.
caveats: Premium tier not included
Network egress and operations may incur charges
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if you exceed 5 GB or use premium features

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure portal.
Create a general-purpose v2 storage account.
Navigate to File shares and create a new file share.
Connect using SMB or NFS from your client or VM.
Upload or access files as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need managed, cloud-based file shares accessible via SMB or NFS for applications or users.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require object storage, block storage, or need high-performance workloads not covered by the standard tier.
entry.body: Azure File Storage offers managed file shares in the cloud with SMB and NFS support, suitable for shared storage, migration, and hybrid scenarios.

entry.title: Backblaze B2
entry.description: Backblaze B2 offers affordable, scalable cloud object storage with a generous always-free tier for developers and small projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: 10 GB free storage, 1 GB daily outbound downloads, and unlimited uploads.
caveats: Overages billed at standard rates
API calls and lifecycle rules may incur costs
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding free limits incurs automatic charges

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Backblaze account.
Create a B2 bucket in the dashboard.
Generate an application key for API access.
Upload files via web UI, CLI, or S3-compatible tools.
Configure bucket permissions and lifecycle rules as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Backblaze B2 for affordable, scalable object storage when you need S3 compatibility or simple backups for small to medium workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced CDN features, ultra-low latency, or need to store and serve large volumes of data for free.
entry.body: Backblaze B2 provides cost-effective, S3-compatible object storage with a generous always-free tier, ideal for backups, media, and small-scale cloud storage needs.

entry.title: Bitbucket
entry.description: Bitbucket is a Git-based code hosting and CI/CD platform by Atlassian for collaborative software development.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for small teams with unlimited private repositories and basic CI/CD.
caveats: Build minutes and storage are pooled across all users.
Advanced permissions and premium features require paid plans.
billingRiskNotes: Exceeding build minutes or storage disables further usage until next period or upgrade.

useCases: Host private or public Git repositories
Collaborate on code with pull requests
Automate builds and deployments with Pipelines
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Bitbucket account at https://bitbucket.org/.
Create a new repository or import an existing one.
Clone the repository to your local machine.
Push your code and collaborate using branches and pull requests.
Set up Bitbucket Pipelines for CI/CD if needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Bitbucket for small teams or individuals needing private Git repositories with integrated CI/CD and Atlassian ecosystem integration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced CI/CD, unlimited users, or enterprise-level permissions without upgrading to a paid plan.
avoidIf: organizations with more than 5 users on free tier
entry.body: Bitbucket offers free private repositories and basic CI/CD for small teams, making it suitable for collaborative development and integration with Atlassian tools.

entry.title: Cloud Storage
entry.description: Google Cloud Storage offers scalable, secure object storage for any amount of data with global accessibility.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier includes limited storage, network, and operations per month.
caveats: Free tier applies only to select US regions.
Overages billed at standard rates.
billingRiskNotes: No hard cap; monitor usage to avoid unexpected costs.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Google Cloud account and enable billing.
Navigate to Cloud Storage in the Google Cloud Console.
Create a new storage bucket in a supported US region.
Upload files or configure bucket permissions as needed.
Monitor usage to stay within free tier limits.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need reliable, scalable object storage for static files, backups, or serving assets with global access.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require storage outside US regions for the free tier, or need strict hard caps to prevent overages.
entry.body: Google Cloud Storage provides secure, highly available object storage with an always-free tier for small-scale use in select US regions. It is suitable for hosting static websites, storing backups, and serving media files, but users should monitor usage to avoid overages as there is no hard cap on the free tier.

entry.title: Cloudflare R2
entry.description: Cloudflare R2 is an object storage service with S3-compatible API and no egress fees.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10 GB storage and 1,000,000 read/write operations per month.
caveats: Overages billed at standard rates
No egress fees beyond free tier
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free tier is billed automatically

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Cloudflare account or log in.
Navigate to the R2 dashboard in the Cloudflare console.
Create a new R2 bucket and note the access keys.
Configure your application or tool with the S3-compatible endpoint and credentials.
Upload and manage objects via the dashboard or API.
entry.whenToUse: Use Cloudflare R2 for affordable, S3-compatible object storage with no egress fees, especially for static assets or backups.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced S3 features like Glacier, or require strict hard caps to prevent overages.
entry.body: Cloudflare R2 provides S3-compatible object storage with no egress fees, making it ideal for serving static assets and backups at low cost.

entry.title: Glacier
entry.description: AWS Glacier provides low-cost, long-term archival storage for infrequently accessed data with flexible retrieval options.

freeTierDetails.summary: 10 GB of free retrievals per month for the first 12 months.
limits: Standard S3 Glacier storage fees apply after free tier
caveats: Storage is not free, only retrievals are covered in free tier.
Retrieval times can range from minutes to hours.
billingRiskNotes: Storage and retrievals beyond free tier incur charges.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an AWS account and verify billing.
Navigate to the S3 console and create a new bucket.
Enable Glacier storage class for your bucket or objects.
Upload files to the bucket, selecting Glacier as the storage class.
Set up lifecycle policies for automatic archival if needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Glacier for storing data you rarely access but must retain for years, such as backups or compliance archives.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Glacier if you need frequent or instant access to your data, or if you require low-latency retrieval.
entry.body: AWS Glacier is a cost-effective solution for long-term data archiving, offering secure, durable storage with flexible retrieval options and integration with S3.

entry.title: Oracle Object Storage
entry.description: Oracle Object Storage provides scalable, secure cloud storage for unstructured data, supporting backup, archiving, and application data needs.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier includes 10 GB of standard storage and 10,000 GET/PUT requests per month.
caveats: Overages are billed at standard rates
Requires Oracle Cloud Free Tier account

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an Oracle Cloud Free Tier account.
Navigate to the Object Storage service in the Oracle Cloud Console.
Create a new bucket and set access permissions.
Upload files or connect via S3-compatible tools.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need reliable, S3-compatible cloud storage for backups, archives, or serving static assets with a generous free tier.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need high-throughput, multi-region replication, or advanced CDN integration not natively supported by Oracle.
entry.body: Oracle Object Storage offers always-free cloud storage with S3 compatibility, ideal for backups, archives, and static content with simple setup and management.

entry.title: S3
entry.description: Amazon S3 provides scalable object storage for files, backups, and static website hosting.

freeTierDetails.summary: 5 GB Standard Storage, 20,000 GET and 2,000 PUT requests monthly for 12 months.
limits: 2,000 PUT, COPY, POST, or LIST requests/month
caveats: Free tier valid for 12 months after account creation
Charges apply after limits or period end
billingRiskNotes: Overages are billed automatically after free tier limits or 12 months

quickstartSteps: Sign up for an AWS account and log in to the AWS Management Console.
Navigate to the S3 service and create a new bucket.
Configure bucket settings, including region and access permissions.
Upload files or objects to your bucket.
Set up bucket policies or static website hosting as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use S3 for scalable, reliable object storage or static website hosting with pay-as-you-go pricing and global availability.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid S3 for low-latency block storage, high-frequency transactional databases, or if you need always-free storage beyond 12 months.
entry.body: Amazon S3 is a widely used object storage service for storing and retrieving any amount of data at scale, suitable for static sites, backups, and more.

entry.title: Supabase Storage
entry.description: Supabase Storage provides scalable object storage with access controls and integration with Supabase projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1 GB storage and 2 GB bandwidth per project per month.
caveats: Overages billed automatically
No custom domain support on free tier
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free limits incurs charges automatically

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Supabase account and create a new project.
Navigate to the 'Storage' section in your project dashboard.
Create a new storage bucket and configure access policies.
Use the Supabase client libraries to upload and retrieve files.
Monitor usage and adjust permissions as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need simple, integrated object storage for files in Supabase-backed apps with managed access controls.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require large-scale storage, advanced CDN features, or strict hard caps to prevent overages.
entry.body: Supabase Storage offers integrated, developer-friendly object storage for files and media, with free tier limits suitable for small projects and prototyping.
